message:
refactor(installer): resolve binaries from target-owned paths instead of inherited PATH

Across the install, doctor, stack, smoke, info, agents, update, and services-setup
libraries we previously relied on `command -v <tool>` / `command_exists` to decide
whether a managed CLI (bun, uv, cargo, go, atuin, zoxide, lazygit, lazydocker, gh,
psql, vault, claude, codex, gemini, ntm, sg, lsd, dust, tldr, am, …) was already
installed. That is unsafe in the contexts where these scripts run:

  * `install.sh` and its phased helpers run as root via sudo while managing files
    owned by TARGET_USER. Root's PATH frequently contains tools that the target
    user does not actually have installed into `$TARGET_HOME`, producing false
    positives that caused the installer to skip per-user bootstrap (uv, atuin,
    cargo, etc.).
  * `scripts/services-setup.sh`, `scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh`, and the update path
    may be invoked from shells whose PATH leaks tools out of the invoker's home.
    The resulting "already installed" decisions then configured systemd units or
    shell rcs that pointed at binaries the target user cannot read.
  * `scripts/lib/doctor*.sh` and `scripts/lib/stack.sh` resolve `am` (MCP Agent
    Mail CLI) in several places. The old code fell back to `command -v am`, which
    found whatever `am` happened to be on the current shell's PATH (often the
    Rust build of a developer-shell user) rather than the managed install under
    `$TARGET_HOME/mcp_agent_mail/am` / `$TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/am`.

This change introduces a consistent set of binary-resolution helpers that only
trust target-owned or stable system locations:

    install.sh                : binary_path / binary_installed
    scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh  : _cli_target_has_command
    scripts/lib/stack.sh      : _stack_target_has_command (+ consolidated
                                _stack_agent_mail_cli_path candidate list)
    scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh : _smoke_binary_path / _smoke_binary_exists
    scripts/lib/doctor.sh     : doctor_binary_path / doctor_binary_exists
                                and doctor_agent_mail_cli_path
    scripts/lib/doctor_fix.sh : doctor_fix_agent_mail_cli_path and
                                doctor_fix_resolve_path_target
    scripts/lib/info.sh       : info_binary_path
    scripts/lib/agents.sh     : _agent_find_am_bin / _agent_detect_am_mcp_path
    scripts/lib/update.sh     : update_binary_path / update_binary_exists,
                                plus target-home fallbacks in
                                update_preferred_user_bin_dir and
                                update_default_user_bin_dir
    scripts/services-setup.sh : find_user_bin now also checks /usr/bin, /bin,
                                /snap/bin so system packages like bash/psql are
                                resolvable under `set -u` with a bare PATH

Each helper searches, in order: `$ACFS_BIN_DIR` (or `$ACFS_HOME/bin`), then
`$TARGET_HOME/.local/bin`, `$TARGET_HOME/.acfs/bin`, `$TARGET_HOME/.bun/bin`,
`$TARGET_HOME/.cargo/bin`, `$TARGET_HOME/.atuin/bin`, `$TARGET_HOME/go/bin`,
`$TARGET_HOME/bin`, then the stable system paths `/usr/local/bin`, `/usr/bin`,
`/bin`, `/usr/local/sbin`, `/usr/sbin`, `/sbin`, `/snap/bin`. The caller's
arbitrary PATH is no longer consulted, so a stale entry can no longer short-
circuit installation or drive the installer to wire up the wrong binary path.

Follow-on behavior changes enabled by the new helpers:

  * `get_version_line` in doctor.sh now resolves the binary first and invokes
    that absolute path for `--version`, so version reporting matches the
    install that the rest of the installer is actually managing.
  * `_configure_gemini_settings` in agents.sh selects the MCP base path
    (`/mcp/` for the Rust `am`, `/api/` for the Python `mcp_agent_mail`) based
    on the resolved target-home binary rather than the current shell's `am`,
    and the "needs update" conditionals treat any mismatch against the
    expected auth / MCP URL as a repair trigger instead of only `unset` /
    `error`.
  * `_stack_agent_mail_ready` now refuses to run the readiness probe unless a
    target-owned `am` resolves, and shell-quotes the resolved path into the
    heredoc instead of re-searching from the probe's own PATH.
  * `install_atuin` in cli_tools.sh keys off the concrete target binary path
    (`$TARGET_HOME/.atuin/bin/atuin`) so a stray `~/.atuin` directory on the
    caller cannot cause the installer to skip the per-user install.

Tests updated/added:

  * tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats — new cases asserting that
    `get_version` and `update_preferred_user_bin_dir` prefer binaries under
    `$TARGET_HOME/.bun/bin`, `.cargo/bin`, `.local/bin` over an equally valid
    binary in the invoker's own `$HOME`. Adds `unset` of TARGET_USER/HOME/
    ACFS_BIN_DIR/ACFS_STATE_FILE/ACFS_HOME in setup() so each test starts
    from a known baseline.
  * tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh — new cases for
    `find_user_bin` (confirms system paths /usr/bin, /bin are searched),
    `_stack_is_installed` handling of unknown tools under `set -u`, and the
    behavior that stack helpers ignore current-shell-only PATH entries.
  * tests/unit/test_doctor_fix.sh — adds `sudo() { "$@"; }` shims where the
    doctor fixer now routes through sudo, plus a new case asserting that the
    agent-mail unit writer prefers `$TARGET_HOME/mcp_agent_mail/am` over an
    unrelated `am` on the current shell's PATH.
  * tests/unit/test_doctor_generated.sh — widens the fix-suggestion regex to
    match only install-style `curl -fsSL …` hints, so it no longer breaks
    when earlier checks emit manual repair hints like `am doctor repair --yes`.

No functional behavior change for installs that were already correctly resolved
via target-owned paths; this tightens the false-positive and false-negative
paths that arose when PATH was inherited from an arbitrary caller (sudo, a
developer shell, a cron harness).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -5043,13 +5045,39 @@ install_cloud_db() {
 # Phase 8: Dicklesworthstone stack
 # ============================================================
 
-# Helper: check if a binary exists in common install locations
-binary_installed() {
+# Resolve binaries only from target-owned or stable system locations.
+# Do not trust arbitrary inherited PATH entries here: they can point at tools from
+# the caller's shell instead of the target installation we are managing.
+binary_path() {
     local name="$1"
-    [[ -x "$ACFS_BIN_DIR/$name" ]] || \
-    [[ -x "/usr/local/bin/$name" ]] || \
-    [[ -x "$TARGET_HOME/.bun/bin/$name" ]] || \
-    [[ -x "$TARGET_HOME/.cargo/bin/$name" ]]
+    local primary_bin="${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-$TARGET_HOME/.local/bin}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    for candidate in \
+        "$primary_bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/.local/bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/.acfs/bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/.cargo/bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/.bun/bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/.atuin/bin/$name" \
+        "$TARGET_HOME/go/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/local/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/bin/$name" \
+        "/bin/$name" \
+        "/snap/bin/$name"; do
+        if [[ -x "$candidate" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+binary_installed() {
+    local path=""
+    path="$(binary_path "$1" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$path" ]]
 }

+        resolve_target_am() {

+

 # Post-install smoke test
 # Runs quick, automatic verification at the end of install.sh
 # ============================================================
+_smoke_target_path() {
+    local user_home="${TARGET_HOME:-}"
+    if [[ -z "$user_home" ]]; then
+        user_home="$(acfs_home_for_user "$TARGET_USER" || true)"
+    fi
+    if [[ -z "$user_home" ]] || [[ "$user_home" != /* ]]; then
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    printf '%s\n' "${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-$user_home/.local/bin}:$user_home/.local/bin:$user_home/.acfs/bin:$user_home/.cargo/bin:$user_home/.bun/bin:$user_home/.atuin/bin:$user_home/go/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/snap/bin"
+}

-    fi
-    run_as_target bash -c "$cmd"

 }

-    elif command_exists vault; then

         echo "✅ Vault: installed" >&2
     else
         echo "⚠️ Vault: not installed (optional)" >&2

@@ -187,6 +187,54 @@ _agent_check_bun() {
     return 0
 }
 
+_agent_find_am_bin() {
+    local target_home="${1:-}"
+    local primary_bin=""
+    local candidate=""
+
+    if [[ -z "$target_home" ]]; then
+        target_home="$(_agent_target_home "${TARGET_USER:-ubuntu}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+    [[ -n "$target_home" ]] || return 1
+
+    primary_bin="${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-$target_home/.local/bin}"
+    for candidate in \
+        "$target_home/mcp_agent_mail/am" \
+        "$primary_bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/.local/bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/.acfs/bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/.cargo/bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/.bun/bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/.atuin/bin/am" \
+        "$target_home/go/bin/am"; do
+        if [[ -x "$candidate" ]]; then
+            printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+_agent_detect_am_mcp_path() {
+    local target_home="${1:-}"
+    local am_bin=""
+
+    am_bin="$(_agent_find_am_bin "$target_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$am_bin" ]] && "$am_bin" --version 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^am '; then
+        printf '/mcp/\n'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$am_bin" ]]; then
+        printf '/api/\n'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    printf '/mcp/\n'
+}
+

 # This avoids the "node not found" error when nvm hasn't added node to PATH yet.
 # The wrapper is placed in ~/.local/bin which is early in PATH.
@@ -487,11 +535,7 @@ _configure_gemini_settings() {

     local am_mcp_path="/mcp/"
-    if command -v am &>/dev/null; then
-        if ! am --version 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^am '; then
-            am_mcp_path="/api/"
-        fi
-    fi

+@test "get_version: prefers target runtime binaries when HOME differs" {
+    local current_home
+    local target_home
+    current_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    target_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+
+    export HOME="$current_home"
+    export TARGET_USER="ubuntu"
+    export TARGET_HOME="$target_home"
+    unset ACFS_BIN_DIR
+    unset ACFS_STATE_FILE
+    unset ACFS_HOME
+
+    mkdir -p "$current_home/.bun/bin" "$current_home/.cargo/bin" "$current_home/.local/bin"
+    mkdir -p "$target_home/.bun/bin" "$target_home/.cargo/bin" "$target_home/.local/bin"
+
+    cat > "$current_home/.bun/bin/bun" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "0.9.0"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$current_home/.bun/bin/bun"
+
+    cat > "$target_home/.bun/bin/bun" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "1.3.12"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$target_home/.bun/bin/bun"
+
+    cat > "$current_home/.cargo/bin/rustc" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "rustc 1.70.0 (old)"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$current_home/.cargo/bin/rustc"
+
+    cat > "$target_home/.cargo/bin/rustc" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "rustc 1.88.0 (target)"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$target_home/.cargo/bin/rustc"
+
+    cat > "$current_home/.local/bin/uv" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "uv 0.10.0"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$current_home/.local/bin/uv"
+
+    cat > "$target_home/.local/bin/uv" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo "uv 0.11.6"
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$target_home/.local/bin/uv"
+
+    run get_version "bun"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "1.3.12"
+
+    run get_version "rust"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "1.88.0"
+
+    run get_version "uv"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "0.11.6"
+}
